34.20: They shall suddenly die, and at mid-night peoples shall be troubled, & shall pass, & take away the violent without hand.
34.21: For his eyes are upon the ways of men, and he considereth all their steps.
34.22: There is not darkness, & there is not shadow of death, that they may be hid there which work iniquity.
34.23: For it is no more in man's power to come to God into judgment.
34.24: He shall destroy many, & innumerable, & shall make other to stand for them.
34.25: For he knoweth their works: and therefore he shall bring night, and they shall be destroyed.
34.26: As impious men he hath strucken them in the place of them that see.
34.27: Who as it were of purpose have revolted from him, & would not understand all his ways:
34.28: That they might cause the cry of the needy man to come to him, & he hear the voice of the poor.
34.29: For he granting peace, who is there that can condemn? After he shall hide his countenance, who is there that may behold him, both upon nations, and upon all men?
34.30: Who maketh a man that is an hypocrite reign for the sins of the people.
34.31: Therefore because I have spoken to God, thee also I will not prohibit.
34.32: If I have erred, teach thou me: If I have spoken iniquity, I will add no more.
34.33: Doth God require it of thee, because it hath displeased thee? For thou beganst to speak, and not I: but if thou know any better thing, speak.
34.34: Let men of understanding speak to me, and let a wiseman hear me.
34.35: But Job hath spoken foolishly, and his words sound not discipline.
34.36: O my father, let Job be proved even to the end: cease not from the man of iniquity.
34.37: Who addeth blasphemy upon his sins, let him be restrained in the mean time among us: and then let him provoke God to judgment with his speeches.

35.1: Therefore Eliu again spake these words:
35.2: Doth thy cogitation seem just to thee, that thou saidst: I am juster than God?
35.3: For thou saidst: That which is right doth not please thee: or what will it profit thee if I sin?
35.4: Therefore will I answer to thy words, and to thy friends with thee.
35.5: Look up to heaven and see, and behold the sky, that it is higher than thou.
35.6: If thou sin, what shalt thou hurt him? And if thine iniquities be multiplied, what shall thou do against him?
35.7: Moreover if thou do justly, what shalt thou give him, or what shall he receive of thy hand?
35.8: Man that is like to thee, thy impiety shall hurt: and thy justice shall help the son of man.
35.9: Because of the multitude of calumniators they shall cry: and shall wail for the force of the arm of tyrants.
35.10: And he hath not said: Where is God, that made me, that hath given songs in the night?
35.11: Who teacheth us above the beasts of the earth, and instructeth us above the fowls of the air.
35.12: There shall they cry, and he will not hear, because of the pride of the evil.
35.13: God therefore will not hear without cause, and the Omnipotent will behold the causes of every one.
35.14: Yea when thou shalt say: He considereth not: be judged before him, and expect him.
35.15: For he doth not now infer his fury, neither doth he revenge wickedness exceedingly.
35.16: Therefore Job in vain openeth his mouth, and without knowledge multiplieth words.

36.1: Eliu also adding spake these words:
36.2: Bear with me a little, and I will shew to thee: for as yet I have what to speak for God.
36.3: I will repeat my knowledge from the beginning, and I will prove my maker just.
36.4: For indeed my words are without lie, & perfect knowledge shall be proved to thee.
36.5: God doth not cast away the mighty, whereas himself also is mighty.
36.6: But he saveth not the impious, and he giveth judgment to the poor.
36.7: He shall not take away his eyes from the just man, and he placeth kings in the throne for ever, and there they are extolled.
36.8: And if they shall be in chains, & be bound with the ropes of poverty.
36.9: He shall shew them their works, and their wicked deeds, because they have been violent.
36.10: He also shall reveal their ear, to chastise them: & shall speak, that they may return from iniquity:
36.11: If they shall hear and observe, they shall accomplish their days in good, and their years in glory.
36.12: But if they hear not, they shall pass by the sword, and shall be consumed in folly.
36.13: Dissemblers and crafty men provoke the wrath of God, neither shall they cry when they are bound.
36.14: Their soul shall die in tempest, and their life, among the effeminates.
36.15: He shall deliver the poor out of his distress, and shall reveal his ear in tribulation.
36.16: Therefore he shall save thee most largely out of the narrow mouth, and not having foundation under it: and the quietness of thy table shall be full of fatness.
36.17: Thy cause is judged as an impious man's, cause and judgment thou shalt receive.
36.18: Let not therefore anger overcome thee, that thou oppress any man: neither let multitude of gifts incline thee.
36.19: Lay down thy greatness without tribulation, & all the puissant of strength.
36.20: Protract not the night, that peoples may come up for them.
36.21: Beware thou decline not to iniquity: for thou hast begun to follow it after misery.
36.22: Behold, God is high in his strength, and none is like to him among the law givers.
36.23: Who can search his ways? Or who can say to him: Thou hast wrought iniquity?
36.24: Remember that thou knowest not his work, whereof men have sung.
36.25: All men see him, every one beholdeth far off.
36.26: Behold, God is great surmounting our knowledge: the number of his years is inestimable.
36.27: Who taketh away the drops of rain, & poureth out showers as it were gifts of water.
36.28: Which flow out from the clouds, that cover all things from above.
36.29: If he will stretch forth clouds as his tent,
36.30: And lighten with his light from above, he shall cover also the ends of the sea.
36.31: For by these he judgeth peoples, and giveth victuals to many mortal men.
36.32: In his hands he hideth the light, & commandeth it that it come again.
36.33: He sheweth his friend thereof, that it is his possession, and that he may ascend to it.

37.1: Upon this my heart is sore afraid, and is moved out of his place.
37.2: Hear ye his speech in the terror of his voice, and the sound proceeding out of his mouth.
37.3: Under all the heavens he considereth, and his light is upon the ends of the earth.
37.4: After him shall sounding roar, he shall thunder with the voice of his greatness, and shall not be found out when his voice shall be heard.
37.5: God shall thunder in his voice marvelously, he that doeth great & unsearchable things.
37.6: He that commandeth the snow to descend upon the earth, and the winter rains, and the shower of his strength.
37.7: He that signeth in the hand of all men, that every one may know his works.
37.8: The beast shall enter into his covert, and shall abide in his den.
37.9: From the inner parts shall a tempest come forth, & cold from Arcturus.
37.10: When God bloweth frost congealeth, and again waters are poured most largely.
37.11: Corn desireth clouds, & the clouds spread their light.
37.12: Which go round about, whither-soever the will of the governer shall lead them, to all that he shall command them upon the face of the whole earth.
37.13: Whether in one tribe, or in his land, or in what place soever of his mercy he shall command them to be found.
37.14: Harken to these things Job: stand, and consider the marvels of God.
37.15: Doest thou know when God commanded the rains, that they shew the light of his clouds?
37.16: Knowest thou the great paths of the clouds, and the perfect knowledges?
37.17: Are not thy garments hot, when the earth shall be blown with the South wind?
37.18: Thou perhaps madest the heavens with him, which are most sound, cast as it were of brass.
37.19: Shew us what we may say to him: for we are wrapped in darkness.
37.20: Who shall tell him the things that I speak? Yea if man shall speak, he shall be devoured.
37.21: But now they see not the light: suddenly the air shall be thickened into clouds, and the wind passing by shall drive them away.
37.22: From the North gold cometh, & toward God fearful praising.
37.23: We cannot find him worthily: great of strength, and judgment, and justice, and he cannot be uttered.
37.24: Therefore shall men fear him, and all that seem to themselves to be wise, shall not dare to behold him.

38.1: But our Lord answering Job out of a whirlwind, said:
38.2: Who is this that wrappeth in sentences with unskillful words?
38.3: Gird thy loins as a man: I will ask thee, and answer thou me.
38.4: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell me if thou hast understanding.
38.5: Who set the measures thereof, if thou know: or who stretched out the line upon it?
38.6: Upon what are the foundations thereof grounded? Or who let down the corner stone thereof,
38.7: when the morning stars praised me together, and all the sons of God made jubilation?
38.8: Who shut in the sea with doors when it brake forth, proceeding as it were out of a matrice:
38.9: When I made a cloud the garment thereof, and wrapped it in darkness as in clouts of infancy.
38.10: I compassed it with my bounds, and put bar and doors.
38.11: And I said. Hitherto thou shalt come, and shalt not proceed farther, and here thou shalt break thy swelling waves.
38.12: Didst thou after thy birth command the morning, and shew the dawning his place?
38.13: And didst thou hold the extremities of the earth shaking them & hast thou shaken the impious out of it?
38.14: The seal shall be restored as clay, and shall stand as a garment:
38.15: From the impious their light shall be taken away, and the high arm shall be broken.
38.16: Hast thou entered into the depths of the sea, and walked in the lowest parts of the great depth?
38.17: Have the gates of death been open to thee, & hast thou seen the darksome doors?
38.18: Hast thou considered the breadth of the earth? Tell me if thou know all things,
38.19: In what way the light dwelleth, & what is the place of darkness.
38.20: That thou canst bring every thing to his borders, & understand the paths of the house thereof.
38.21: Didst thou know then that thou shouldest be born? & didst thou know the number of thy days?
38.22: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow, or hast thou beheld treasures of hail?
38.23: Which I have prepared for the time of the enemy, for the day of fight & battle?
38.24: What way is the light spread, is heat divided upon the earth?
38.25: Who gave course to the most vehement shower, and the way of the sounding thunder:
38.26: That it should rain upon the earth, without man in the desert, where no mortal man abideth:
38.27: That it should fill the desert and desolate ground, and should bring forth green grass?
38.28: Who is the father of rain? Or who begot the drops of dew?
38.29: Out of whose womb came forth ice? And frost from heaven who engendered?
38.30: Waters are hardened like stone, and the face of the depth is congealed.
38.31: Shalt thou be able to join together the shining stars Pleiades, or canst thou dissipate the circuit of Arcturus?
38.32: Doest thou bring forth the day star in his time, & make the evening star to rise upon the children of the earth?
38.33: Doest thou know the order of heaven, and shalt thou put down the reason thereof on the earth?
38.34: Shalt thou elevate the voice in the cloud, & the violence of the waters cover thee?
38.35: Shalt thou send lightnings, and will they go, and returning shall they say to thee: Here we are?
38.36: Who put wisdom in the heart of man or who gave the cock understanding?
38.37: Who shall declare the manner of the heavens, and the harmony of heaven who shall make to sleep?
38.38: When was the dust poured on the earth, and the clods compact together?
38.39: Shalt thou take a prey for the lioness and fill the appetite of her whelps?
38.40: When they lie in the dens, & in holes sit in wait?
38.41: Who prepareth for the raven her meat, when her young ones cry to God, wandering about, because they have not meat?

39.1: Hast thou known the time when the wild goats bring forth young among the rocks, or hast thou observed the hinds when they fawn?
39.2: Hast thou numbered the months of their conceiving, and known the time of their bearing?
39.3: They bow down themselves to bring forth young, and they cast them, and make roarings.
39.4: Their young are separated, and go to feed: they go forth, and return not to them.
39.5: Who hath dismist the wild ass free, and who hath loosed his bonds?
39.6: To whom I have given a house in the wilderness, and his tabernacles in the land of saltness.
39.7: He contemneth the multitude of the city, the cry of the exactor he heareth not.
39.8: He looketh about the mountains of his pasture, and seeketh out all green places.
39.9: Will the Rhinoceros serve thee, and will he tarry at thy stall?
39.10: Shalt thou tie the Rhinoceros with thy collar to plough, or will he break the clods of the valleys after thee?
39.11: Shalt thou have confidence in his great strength, and leave thy labours unto him?
39.12: Wilt thou credit him that he will render thee the seed, and gather together thy barn floor?
39.13: The wing of the Ostrich is like to the wings of the Herodius, and of the hawk.
39.14: When she leaveth her eggs on the earth, thou perhaps wilt heat them in the dust.
39.15: She forgeteth that foot may tread upon them, or beast of the field break them.
39.16: She is hardened toward her young, as though they were not hers, she hath laboured in vain no fear compelling her.
39.17: For God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he given her understanding.
39.18: When time shall be, she setteth up the wings on high: she scorneth the horse and his rider.
39.19: Shalt thou give strength to the horse, or put neighing about his neck?
39.20: Shalt thou raise him up as Locusts? The glory of his nostrils is terror.
39.21: He diggeth the earth with his hoof, he pranceth boldly, he goeth forward to meet the armed men.
39.22: He contemneth fear, neither yieldeth he to the sword.
39.23: Upon him shall the quiver sound, the spear shall glister and the shield.
39.24: Fervent and fuming he suppeth the earth, neither doth he make account when the noise of the trumpet soundeth.
39.25: When he shall hear the trumpet he saith: Vah, he smelleth battle far off, the exhortation of the captains, and the cry of the army.
39.26: Doth the hawk wax feathered by thy wisdom, spreading her wings to the South?
39.27: Shall the eagle mount at thy commandment, and put her nest in high places?
39.28: She abideth in rocks, and tarrieth among cragged flints, and stony hills where is no access.
39.29: Thence she beholdeth the prey, and her eyes see a far off.
39.30: Her young ones shall lick blood: and wheresoever the carcass shall be, she is present by and by.
39.31: And our Lord added, and spake to Job:
39.32: He that contendeth with God is he quieted so easily? Verily he that reproveth God, ought to answer him.
39.33: But Job answering our Lord, said:
39.34: Ithat have spoken lightly what can I answer? I will put my hand upon my mouth.
39.35: One thing I have spoken, which I would I had not said: and another, to the which things I will add no more.

40.1: And our Lord answering Job out of the whirlwind, said:
40.2: Gird thy loins as a man: I will ask thee, & do thou tell me.
40.3: Shalt thou make my judgment of none effect: and condemn me, that thou mayst be justified?
40.4: And hast thou an arm as God, and dost thou thunder with like voice?
40.5: Put beauty about thee, and set up thyself aloft, and be glorious, and put on goodly garments.
40.6: Disperse the proud in thy fury, and beholding every arrogant man, humble him.
40.7: Behold all the proud, and confound them, and destroy the impious in their place.
40.8: Hide them in the dust together, and plunge their faces in the pit.
40.9: And I will confess, that thy right hand is able to save thee.
40.10: Behold Behemoth whom I made with thee, shall eat hay as it were an oxe.
40.11: His strength is in his loins, and his power in the navel of his belly.
40.12: He gathereth together his tail as the cedar tree, the sinews of his stones are perplex.
40.13: His bones are as pipes of brass, his gristle as it were plates of iron.
40.14: He is the beginning of the ways of God, which made him, he shall apply his sword.
40.15: To him the mountains bear grass: all the beasts of the field shall play there.
40.16: He sleepeth under the shadow, in the secret of the reed, and in moist places.
40.17: Shadows do protect his shadow, the willows of the torrent shall compass him.
40.18: Lo, he shall sup up the river, & shall not marvel: and he hath confidence that Jordan may run into his mouth.
40.19: In his eyes as with a hook he shall take him, and with stakes he shall bore through his nostrils.
40.20: Canst thou draw out the Leviathan with a hook, and with a rope shalt thou tie his tongue?
40.21: Shalt thou put a ring in his nostrils, or bore through his jaw with a buckle?
40.22: Will he multiply prayers to thee, or speak to thee gentle words?
40.23: Will he make a covenant with thee, and shalt thou take him to be a servant for ever?
40.24: Shalt thou delude him as a bird, or tie him for thy handmaids?
40.25: Shall friends cut him, merchants divide him?
40.26: Shalt thou fill nests with his skin, and the cabin of fishes with his head?
40.27: Lay thy hand upon him: remember battle, and add to speak no more.
40.28: Behold his hope shall frustrate him, and in the sight of all he shall be cast down headlong.

41.1: Not as cruel will I raise him: for who can resist my countenance?
41.2: Who hath given me before, that I may render unto him? All things that are under heaven be mine.
41.3: I will not spare him, and his mighty words, and framed to beseech.
41.4: Who shall reveal the face of his garment: and who shall enter into the midst of his mouth?
41.5: Who shall open the gate of his countenance? Dread is round about his teeth.
41.6: His body as shields that are cast, compact with scales fast cleaving together.
41.7: One is joined to another, and not so much as any air entereth between them:
41.8: One shall stick to another, and holding each other, they shall not be separated.
41.9: His sneezing is as the shining of fire, and his eyes as the twinklings of the morning.
41.10: Out of his mouth proceed lamps, as it were torches of lighted fire.
41.11: Out of his nostrils proceedeth smoke, as it were of a pot heated and boiling.
41.12: His breath maketh coals to burn, & a flame cometh forth out of his mouth.
41.13: In his neck shall strength abide, and neediness goeth before his face.
41.14: The members of his flesh cleave together one to another: he shall send lightnings against him, and he shall not be carried to another place.
41.15: His heart shall be hardened as a stone, and shall be stiffly compact as the smith's stithy.
41.16: When he shall be taken away, the Angels shall fear, and being feared shall be purged.
41.17: When the sword shall apprehend him, neither spear, nor breast-plate shall be able to abide.
41.18: For he shall esteem iron as chaff, & brass, as rotten wood.
41.19: The bow-man shall not put him to flight, the stones of the sling, to him are turned into stubble.
41.20: As stubble will he esteem the hammer, and he will laugh him to scorn that shaketh the spear.
41.21: The beams of the sun shall be under him, & he shall strew gold under him as durt.
41.22: He shall make the deep sea to boil as a pot, and shall put it as when ointments boil.
41.23: Apath shall shine after him, he shall esteem the depth as waxing old.
41.24: There is no power upon the earth, that may be compared with him, who is made to fear no man.
41.25: He seeth every high thing, he is king over all the children of pride.

42.1: And Job answering our Lord, said:
42.2: I know that thou canst do all things, and no cogitation is hid from thee.
42.3: Who is this, that concealeth counsel without knowledge? Therefore have I spoken unwisely, and the things that did exceed my knowledge beyond measure.
42.4: Hear (I beseech thee) and I will speak: I will ask thee, and do thou tell me.
42.5: With the hearing of the ear I have heard thee, but now my eye seeth thee.
42.6: Therefore I reprehend myself, and I do penance in imbers and ashes.
42.7: And after our Lord spake these words to Job, he said to Eliphaz the Themanite: My fury is wrath against thee, and against thy two friends, because you have not spoken right before me, as my servant Job.
42.8: Take therefore unto you seven oxen, and seven rams, & go to my servant Job, and offer holocaust for yourselves: and my servant Job shall pray for you: his face I will receive, that the folly be not imputed to you: for you have not spoken right things before me, as my servant Job.
42.9: Eliphaz therefore the Themanite, and Baldad the Suhite, and Sophar the Naamathite went, and did as our Lord had spoken to them, and our Lord received the face of Job.
42.10: Our Lord also was turned at the penance of Job, when he prayed for his friends. And our Lord added all things whatsoever had been Job's, double.
42.11: And all his brethren came to him, and all his sisters, and all that knew him before, and they did eat bread with him in his house: and wagged the head upon him, & comforted him upon all the evil that God had brought in upon him. And every man gave him one ewe, and one earlet of gold.
42.12: And our Lord blessed the last days of Job more than his beginning. And he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.
42.13: And he had seven sons, and three daughters.
42.14: And he called the name of one Dies, and the name of the second Cassia, and the name of the third Cornustibii.
42.15: And there were not found in all the earth women so beautiful as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
42.16: And Job lived after these things, an hundred forty years, and he saw his children, and his children's children, unto the fourth generation, and he died an old man, and full of days.
